final_output:
## Optimizations

### `find_duplicates`

| Original | Optimized |
|---|---|
| O(n²) — nested loops over all pairs | O(n) — single pass |
| Checks `i != j` and `not in duplicates` repeatedly | Uses a `seen` set for O(1) lookups |

**How it works:** Iterate once. If an item is already in `seen`, it's a duplicate — append it. Otherwise, add it to `seen`.

### `count_occurrences`

| Original | Optimized |
|---|---|
| O(t × w) — splits text *and* iterates all words for every search word | O(t + w) — splits once, counts with `Counter`, then looks up each word |

**How it works:** `Counter(text.split())` builds a frequency map in one pass over the text. Then a dict comprehension pulls the count for each requested word (defaulting to `0` if absent).

function.name: file_edit

steps.content:
## Optimizations

### `find_duplicates`

| Original | Optimized |
|---|---|
| O(n²) — nested loops over all pairs | O(n) — single pass |
| Checks `i != j` and `not in duplicates` repeatedly | Uses a `seen` set for O(1) lookups |

**How it works:** Iterate once. If an item is already in `seen`, it's a duplicate — append it. Otherwise, add it to `seen`.

### `count_occurrences`

| Original | Optimized |
|---|---|
| O(t × w) — splits text *and* iterates all words for every search word | O(t + w) — splits once, counts with `Counter`, then looks up each word |

**How it works:** `Counter(text.split())` builds a frequency map in one pass over the text. Then a dict comprehension pulls the count for each requested word (defaulting to `0` if absent).
